# Integrated Sports-Finance Dynamic Decision System for the Golden State Warriors

**Team Member / Core Modeler & Visualization Lead** | 2026 Interdisciplinary Contest in Modeling (ICM)

**TL;DR:** Won the Honorable Mention (H Prize) globally. Responsible for core mathematical modeling, data visualization, and full English typesetting. Designed the "Integrated Sports-Finance Dynamic Decision System (ISFD)" for the NBA's Golden State Warriors to balance athletic performance with the owner's financial profits.

## 🎯 Situation & Task

Problem D of the 2026 ICM required participants to take the perspective of a team owner or general manager and build a quantitative business and management decision model for a professional sports team to navigate league constraints, market expansion, and core player injuries. As the core modeler and typesetter, my task was to mathematically abstract complex financial statements, advanced player metrics, and market environments to construct a multi-objective dynamic decision model, ultimately delivering a 25-page, beautifully visualized English paper that met top-tier international academic standards.

## 💻 Tech Stack

Python, Multi-Objective Programming (MOP), Monte Carlo Simulation, Linear Regression, Data Visualization (Matplotlib / Seaborn), LaTeX / Word Advanced Typesetting

## 🛠️ Core Contributions (Action)

* **Multi-Dimensional Leverage Decision Model:** Abstracted and normalized indices across four dimensions—financial ($S_{FPI}$), competitive ($S_{TEI}$), market ($S_{GDP}$), and structural pressure ($K_t$)—to derive a comprehensive "Leverage Ratio". Combined with financial red lines and owner preference coefficients, I built a prioritized piecewise decision system that mapped continuous data into discrete management directives (e.g., De-leverage, Smart Expansion).


* **MOP Player Strategy & Injury Risk Simulation:** Constructed a comprehensive utility function integrating Player Efficiency Rating (PER), Value Over Replacement Player (VORP), age decay, and commercial value. Under the hard constraints of salary caps and roster stability, I applied Multi-Objective Programming (MOP) to calculate the most cost-effective acquisition strategies. Furthermore, I utilized **Monte Carlo Simulation** to quantify the stochastic impact of core player injuries (e.g., Stephen Curry) on win rates and ticket revenues, precisely calculating the "Marginal Amplification Effect" between 10 to 41 missed games to provide a quantitative basis for triggering "Strategic Tanking".


* **Dynamic Ticket Pricing & High-Level Visualization:** Applied linear regression to analyze the macro impact of win rates on ticket prices and innovatively introduced micro-dynamic multipliers based on opponent heat, date premiums, and roster availability. For the final presentation, I independently handled the full English typesetting and generated highly professional and intuitive data visualizations (e.g., cost-benefit matrices, dynamic leverage trend lines, sensitivity analysis heatmaps), transforming complex mathematical logic into compelling business intelligence.



## 🏆 Results & Impact

* Stood out among tens of thousands of teams globally to win the **Honorable Mention in MCM/ICM**.
* Successfully built the logically rigorous ISFD closed-loop system, simulating 6 dimensions of market diversion effects for league expansions in cities like Seattle and Las Vegas, precisely estimating potential profit loss percentages.


* Delivered a well-typeset, 25-page English paper with a complete logical chain, providing a high-quality strategic memo for the Warriors' management to achieve a win-win in financial resilience and competitive dominance in the heavy-tax era.
